title: lessertia frutescens subsp. microphylla
intro: Lessertia frutescens subsp. microphylla, also known as Cape flax or bushman's poison, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its slender stems and small, yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Lessertia frutescens subsp. microphylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic and antipyretic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
intro_study: According to "International journal of molecular sciences", Lessertia frutescens subsp. microphylla benefits from the use of plant-growth-promoting rhizobacteria isolate TUTLFNC37, which can improve yield, drought tolerance, and the production of secondary metabolites such as phenolics, triterpenes, and antioxidant activity under drought conditions.
